channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Mamdani’s First City-Owned Grocery Store Planned for South Bronx
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani plans to announce that the store will open in the Hunts Point neighborhood next year. Some merchants are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:credit: David Dee Delgado for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The proposed location of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s first city-owned grocery store in the Hunts Point neighborhood of the Bronx.

item.title: Commuters Brace for Chaos Amid Long Island Rail Strike
item.description: Passengers who take the Long Island Rail Road braced for disruptions to their Monday commute after workers walked off the job during failed contract negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 18, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: EE. UU. y China comparten un sentimiento ante la IA
item.description: El futuro tecnológico está tomando forma a una velocidad vertiginosa, pero lo que promete no es para todos.
item.dc:creator: Yi-Ling Liu

item.title: Word of the Day: efficacy
item.description: This word has appeared in 175 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Americans Are Among Those Affected by Ebola Outbreak, C.D.C. Says
item.description: A small number of U.S. citizens have been directly affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, the C.D.C. said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
United States Agency for International Development

media:content.media:description: People wait near an ambulance at a hospital in Bunia, Congo on Sunday.

item.title: Oil Prices Edge Higher as Cease-Fire Remains Tenuous
item.description: Oil prices rose after President Trump issued a new warning to Iran, calling into question the tenuous cease-fire in the war.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Tech Workers Have Fears About A.I., Too. They Can Do Something About It.
item.description: Even the developers of A.I. want regulation. They’re starting to take action.
item.dc:creator: Kate Andrias

item.title: ‘In the City,’ and 7 More Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new Bravo show with “Summer House” alums premieres. And a documentary on Kylie Minogue airs.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Lindsay Hubbard, center, who appeared on “Summer House,” is starring in a new reality series, “In the City.”

item.title: 2 Teenagers Arrested After Drive-By Shootings in Austin Injure at Least 4
item.description: The teenage boys were among three suspects taken into custody in connection with at least 12 shootings across the Texas capital. There was no apparent motive, the mayor said.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:description: Austin Police Chief Lisa Davis speaking alongside Austin Mayor Kirk Watson, right, during a news conference on Sunday.

item.title: Los comentarios de Trump sobre la economía revelan su desconexión de la realidad
item.description: El presidente nunca ha pretendido ser un estadounidense de a pie, pero una reciente “verdad incómoda” lo ha expuesto a críticas de que no comprende la tensión económica de su guerra en Irán.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green

media:content.media:description: Donald Trump hizo unas declaraciones el martes en la Casa Blanca, antes de partir para su viaje a China.

item.title: Catastrophe Is Emerging in the World’s Most Vulnerable Places
item.description: The humanitarian relief system, decimated by cuts, faces a grave challenge as the Middle East war causes soaring costs for food, fuel and fertilizer.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman and Finbarr O’Reilly
item.category: United States Agency for International Development
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Muslima Ibrahim Mohamed, 38, holds her 2-day-old son, Noor Mohamed.

item.title: A French Soccer Star Faces Off Against a Surging Foe: The Far Right
item.description: Kylian Mbappé, the captain of France’s national team, set off a storm a month before the World Cup by suggesting that the National Rally party was a threat to France.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

media:content.media:description: “I know what it means, and what kind of consequences it can have for my country when those kinds of people take control,” Kylian Mbappé said of the French far-right in a magazine interview.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Judith Barnard, Part of a Couple Who Wrote Best Sellers, Is Dead at 94
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, May 18, 2026.

item.title: A Hunger Crisis in Somalia Is Being Worsened by the Middle East War
item.description: Widespread malnutrition in Somalia, initially caused by drought, has been compounded from cuts to humanitarian aid and rising prices driven by the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:credit: Finbarr O'Reilly for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Abdullahi Abdi Abdirahman with his children beneath their tent in Dollow. They traveled there seeking aid from international relief organizations.

item.title: What to Know About the Latest Ebola Outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization declared a global health emergency after about 80 people died from the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: A motorcycle taxi driver at a hospital in Goma, Democratic Republic of Congo, on Sunday.

item.title: Eager for Arms Deal, Taiwan Stresses Need for U.S. Support
item.description: After President Trump hinted that weapons sales to Taiwan could figure in negotiations with China, officials emphasized their island’s strategic importance.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim

media:content.media:description: President Lai Ching-te of Taiwan on Sunday. He called Taiwan a guardian of “peace and stability.”

item.title: Beverley Martyn, Whose Folk Music Breakthrough Took 50 Years, Dies at 79
item.description: As a rising star of the 1960s, she dated Paul Simon, brooded with Nick Drake and drew Barbra Streisand’s envy. Then she sank into an abusive marriage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub

media:content.media:description: Beverley Martyn in 1999. As a promising young singer, she performed at the Monterey Pop Festival in 1967 and met stars like Peter Fonda and Janis Joplin. But she put her career on hold for decades.

item.title: NextEra Energy Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Dominion, Creating a Utility Giant
item.description: A deal, involving the utilities in Florida and Virginia, would come as demand for power is soaring, largely because of the rapid growth of A.I. data centers.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch and Ivan Penn

media:content.media:description: A wind farm owned by NextEra Energy in Texas. Peak electricity demand in coming summers and winters is expected to rise more than 20 percent nationwide by 2035.

item.title: Isabel Leonard and Others Offer Tastings of ‘Frida y Diego’
item.description: The performers of a Met Opera production about Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera have been promoting the show in decidedly unoperatic places, including a cemetery.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.category: El Ultimo Sueno de Frida y Diego (Opera)

media:content.media:description: Isabel Leonard as Frida Kahlo in a rehearsal of the opera “El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego.” This excerpt was an intermission entertainment at a Met Live program of “Eugene Onegin” at movie theaters.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 18, 2026
item.description: Can I hear a little commotion for Rena Cohen’s latest puzzle?
item.dc:creator: Nikita Richardson

media:content.media:description: Pet with caution.

item.title: Peter G. Neumann, Who Warned of Computer Security Risks, Dies at 93
item.description: For decades, he criticized the industry’s lax attitudes toward both computer security and individual digital privacy. And he developed solutions.
item.dc:creator: John Markoff

media:content.media:description: Dr. Peter G. Neumann in 2012 outside his office at SRI International. At 93, he was still working full time on advanced computer security design, which is being adopted by companies such as Google and Microsoft.

item.title: The Quest for Clean Hydrogen Moves Underground
item.description: The dream of clean hydrogen has tantalized energy experts for years, but producing it has been tough. Many start-ups think the answer could lie beneath our feet.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer and Ian Willms

item.title: Conspiracy Trial Will Test Trump’s Aggressive Tactics Against Protesters
item.description: Prosecutors in Spokane, Wash., are trying three activists who they say conspired to impede federal officers. Legal experts call it a stretch.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin

media:content.media:description: Protests against the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown have taken place across the country, including in Spokane, Wash. and Seattle (above).

item.title: He’s Forging the Future of War
item.description: Meet the man who wants Ukraine to use autonomous drones that could decide on their own to use lethal force.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov, Ukraine’s defense minister. is pushing the military to adopt technology more quickly.

item.title: Two Military Jets Collide in Midair at Idaho Air Show
item.description: Video shared on social media showed the jets striking each other and spinning toward the ground. Four crew members safely ejected before the planes crashed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz and Edgar Sandoval
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
Gunfighter Skies Mountain Home Air Force Base

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Air Force photo by Senior Airman Xavier Wilson
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Air Force demonstration squadron Thunderbirds at the Gunfighter Skies air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho on Sunday. Two EA-18G Growler jets that were performing in a demonstration collided.

item.title: How a Jared Polis Came to Release Election Denier Tina Peters from Prison
item.description: Gov. Jared Polis’s decision to commute the sentence of Tina Peters came after months of tense discussions, including one with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Jack Healy

media:content.media:credit: Natalie Behring for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tina Peters speaking at a Trump rally in Wyoming in 2022.

item.title: Review: In ‘Dad Don’t Read This’ a Playwright (Maybe?) Grows Up
item.description: Eliya Smith’s disturbing teen dramedy explores the ambivalence and confusion of life on the brink of adulthood.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

item.title: Elecciones en Perú: Keiko Fujimori y Roberto Sánchez van a segunda vuelta
item.description: Las elecciones presidenciales enfrentan a Keiko Fujimori, conservadora, y Roberto Sánchez, político de izquierda, tras una crisis marcada por retrasos y protestas.
item.dc:creator: Mitra Taj and Genevieve Glatsky

media:content.media:credit: Sara Wayra para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Keiko Fujimori, líder del partido Fuerza Popular, en un acto de campaña el mes pasado en Lima, Perú.

item.title: Colombia recibe a avituristas usuarios de eBird y Merlin
item.description: Colombia alberga la mayor cantidad de especies de aves conocidas por los ornitólogos. La aplicación Merlin está ayudando a desarrollar una industria de “aviturismo” en el país.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak and Esteban Vanegas

media:content.media:description: Samantha Giraldo y Bibiana Acevedo buscan aves en la reserva natural Los Guácharos, en Jardín, Colombia.

item.title: What to Know About Letlow And Fleming in the Louisiana Senate Race
item.description: He was defeated in the Republican primary in Louisiana on Saturday. Representative Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming are now in a runoff for the party’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Julia Letlow at her election watch party in Baton Rouge, La.

item.title: Cassidy Loses Primary After Drawing Trump’s Ire
item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy lost his Louisiana primary on Saturday after President Trump targeted him for voting to impeach him in 2021. The two-term senator took veiled swipes at the president in his concession speech.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Emily Kask for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Bill Cassidy speaking at a watch party in Baton Rouge on Saturday after losing his primary contest.

item.title: La OMS declara el brote de ébola como emergencia de salud internacional
item.description: La magnitud del brote podría ser mucho mayor de lo que se ha detectado y comunicado hasta ahora, dijo la OMS. No hay vacuna ni tratamientos aprobados para la cepa.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang, Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: Un hombre es transportado desde una ambulancia a su llegada el lunes a un hospital tras la confirmación de un brote de ébola en la provincia de Ituri de la República Democrática del Congo.

item.title: Venezuela extradita a Alex Saab, empresario cercano a Maduro, a EE. UU.
item.description: La extradición del magnate multimillonario, vinculado a una enorme trama de corrupción, forma parte de una purga de figuras poderosas que ayudaron al presidente depuesto a mantenerse en el poder.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero

media:content.media:description: Alex Saab, en el centro, en Caracas, Venezuela, en 2023, fue uno de los principales aliados del depuesto presidente venezolano, Nicolás Maduro.

item.title: Chicken Paprikash, Generously Buttered Egg Noodles and a Cucumber Salad
item.description: There’s your Sunday supper, courtesy of Sam Sifton’s five-star recipe.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Clark

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini.
media:content.media:description: Sam Sifton’s chicken paprikash.

item.title: Runoff in Peru Offers Two Starkly Differing Visions for the Nation
item.description: The presidential election pits Keiko Fujimori, a conservative, against Roberto Sánchez, a leftist, after a crisis marked by delays and protests.
item.dc:creator: Mitra Taj and Genevieve Glatsky

media:content.media:credit: Sara Wayra for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Keiko Fujimori, the leader of the Popular Force Party, at a campaign rally last month in Lima, Peru.

item.title: Straus Family Creamery Issues Voluntary Ice Cream Recall
item.description: Straus Family Creamery asked customers to throw out some tubs sold in May across 17 states. No injuries have been reported, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands

media:content.media:description: Straus Family Creamery voluntarily recalled some tubs of its organic ice cream, including strawberry pints and quarts, which were on sale in May.

item.title: What the Dickens? Will Ferrell Plays Ghost of Epstein on ‘S.N.L.’ Season Finale
item.description: Ferrell, the “Saturday Night Live” alum, appears with James Austin Johnson as President Trump in a sendup of “A Christmas Carol.” Paul McCartney provides the music.
item.dc:creator: Dave Itzkoff

item.title: Five Weeknight Dishes, Now on Sundays
item.description: I’m here to help you plan some very delicious (and doable) dinners for your week — hoisin garlic noodles, anyone?
item.dc:creator: Emily Weinstein

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Vanni for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini.

item.title: Getting Jeff Bezos to the Opera
item.description: Box office is waning. Donors are fading. Opera needs new lifeblood. The answer lies with tech fortunes.
item.dc:creator: John Berry

item.title: The Great Disruption: How A.I. Will Shape Our Future
item.description: Readers discuss ways in which artificial intelligence is reshaping our lives. Also: Frequent redistricting.

item.title: Sex, Death and ‘Devils’ at Cannes
item.description: With the main slate making few waves, the biggest moments at the festival so far involve a new horror comedy and a restored horror provocation.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan
item.category: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma (Movie)

media:content.media:description: The director Jane Schoenbrun, center, shares a moment at Cannes with Gillian Anderson, left, and Hannah Einbinder, stars of “Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma.”

item.title: Strong Storms and Tornadoes Forecast for Nebraska, Kansas and Other States
item.description: Nebraska, Iowa and Kansas face the greatest risk of extreme weather, with the worst expected on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: Good Luck, Grads!
item.description: Is the post-college job market as bad as it seems?
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick

media:content.media:credit: KT Kanazawich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Commencement at Johns Hopkins University last year.

item.title: Here’s the Trade Deal That Trump and Xi Should Have Reached
item.description: For this to happen, however, both men would need personality transplants.
item.dc:creator: Soumaya Keynes and Chad P. Bown

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Ribar for The New York Times

item.title: Can We Reverse Aging?
item.description: Inside the billion-dollar bet on cellular rejuvenation.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Abrams, Luke Vander Ploeg, Tina Antolini, Wendy Dorr, Rowan Niemisto, Dan Powell and Marion Lozano

item.title: Wave of Ukrainian Strikes Kills at Least 4, Russia Says
item.description: More than 550 drones were intercepted or shot down in more than a dozen regions, including Moscow, in one of largest attacks of the war.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Cassandra Vinograd

media:content.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A damaged residential building in Zelenograd, in Russia’s Moscow region, after a drone strike on Sunday.

item.title: We Need More ‘Rabid Partisans’ Like Shane Massey
item.description: Defeating a sclerotic rump of a party is no achievement. Instead, one-party rule enables corruption. It fosters stagnation.
item.dc:creator: David French

media:content.media:credit: Tracy Glantz/The Island Packet, via TNS, Abaca Press, via Associated Press

item.title: NYT Connections Answers for May 18, 2026
item.description: Scroll down for hints and conversation about the puzzle for Monday, May 18, 2026.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Demon Attacks. Teleportation. Astrology. The Supernatural Is Back.
item.description: One of the oldest and most durable features of human experience is re-emerging.
item.dc:creator: Katya Ungerman
